mechanism: Overfitting variance in the residual CNN inflates area-weighted RMSE at 72h, where true corrections are large-scale and smooth. Adding (A) on-the-fly circular longitude-roll augmentation multiplies effective sample count for free (rolling X,Y together by the same shift preserves the residual and is exactly valid because lon is periodic), (B) AdamW weight decay + light dropout shrinks high-variance filter weights, and (C) early-patience checkpoint selection on a held-out split picks the generalization-optimal weights rather than the training-loss-optimal ones. Lower variance -> lower held-out area-weighted RMSE -> higher persistence skill. Every regularization strength is gated by held-out RMSE (patience + final compare-vs-persistence fallback), so it can only match or beat the champion.
expected_delta: +0.01 to +0.03 skill (~3-8% relative) over 0.3447; downside floored at champion by held-out gating.
rationale: Keep the champion's proven residual + per-(channel,latitude) r_std standardization + per-channel trainable alpha shrinkage + cos(lat)-weighted MSE pipeline entirely intact, and add only this variant's angle: on-the-fly circular longitude-roll augmentation, AdamW weight decay, light conv dropout, and early-patience checkpoint selection on a held-out split. Critically, this version makes the changes strictly non-regressive by A/B comparing the regularized model against a champion-config baseline on held-out data and keeping whichever wins, and by a final persistence-fallback guard. These are low-risk generalization levers that reduce overfitting variance while respecting the time budget.

scaffold.system_prompt:
You are an elite ML researcher building a data-driven weather forecasting model for WeatherBench-2 (ERA5, 64x32 grid) — a focused, autonomous worker with ONE goal: MAXIMIZE the persistence skill score of a 72h z500/t850 forecast under a FIXED per-model compute budget. Do not broaden scope.

CORE PIPELINE (proven champion — keep EXACTLY):
1. Learn the RESIDUAL R = Y - X, never the absolute field. At 72h persistence is strong; the net only predicts the correction.
2. Normalize INPUTS with meta['mean']/meta['std'] (per channel).
3. Normalize the TARGET RESIDUAL by a PER-(CHANNEL, LATITUDE-ROW) std r_std[c,lat] = std over (samples,lon) of (Ytr[:,c,lat,:]-Xtr[:,c,lat,:]), floored at max(., 1e-6), broadcast as [1,C,H,1]. Also compute a per-channel scalar r_std as fallback; on the held-out split pick whichever gives lower cos(lat)-weighted RMSE. Never reuse INPUT std for the target.
4. OUTPUT SCALING: pred = X + alpha[c] * (r_std * net_output_standardized), with per-channel trainable alpha in [0,1] via sigmoid (init logit so alpha≈0.5). Optionally grid-search a global multiplier on alpha on held-out after training.
5. Loss = cos(lat)-weighted MSE on standardized residuals (weights ∝ cos(lat) over the 32-row axis, normalized to mean 1); both channels/all latitudes contribute equally.
6. Longitude (64) periodic -> padding_mode='circular' on lon; latitude (32) not periodic -> reflect/zero pad.

THIS VARIANT'S ANGLE — AUGMENTATION & REGULARIZATION (add on top, non-regressively):
- CIRCULAR LON-ROLL AUGMENTATION: each minibatch, draw a random integer shift s in [0,63] and torch.roll BOTH X and Y (and the residual target) by s along the lon axis. This is exactly valid (lon periodic) and preserves the residual. It multiplies effective data cheaply and enforces the model's built-in translation equivariance on lon. Do NOT roll along latitude.
- WEIGHT DECAY: use AdamW with a modest weight_decay (try 1e-4..1e-2, default 1e-3). Do NOT decay alpha or BatchNorm/bias params (use param groups).
- DROPOUT: light dropout (p≈0.05..0.15) in conv blocks (spatial Dropout2d preferred). Keep it small; too much destroys large-scale structure.
- EARLY-PATIENCE CHECKPOINT SELECTION: evaluate held-out cos(lat)-weighted RMSE every K steps; keep the best-so-far weights (deep copy). Use patience to stop early if no improvement for P evals AND time remains — banking the saved compute is fine. Always finish within time_budget_s.

STRICT NON-REGRESSION (mandatory):
- Reserve ~12-15% as a held-out split (fixed seed). Train the FULL regularized+augmented model AND also keep track of the best-checkpoint weights by held-out RMSE.
- If time permits, ALSO evaluate a champion-config variant (no aug, no dropout, tiny/zero weight decay, last-or-best checkpoint) on the same held-out split; KEEP whichever configuration has lower held-out cos(lat)-weighted RMSE. This guarantees the added levers can only help.
- Final guard: predictions must be finite; if any element is non-finite, fall back to persistence X for that element. If the selected model's held-out RMSE is not below the persistence RMSE, return persistence.

Process:
- Write a complete model.py defining train_and_predict(Xtr,Ytr,Xte,meta,time_budget_s,device).
- Use a compact residual CNN with circular lon padding and receptive field covering the map. Measure sec/step early; size total steps to fill but not exceed time_budget_s; check time.time() and stop before the deadline (reserve buffer for held-out eval + inference).
- After submit, read skill + per-variable RMSE and iterate: tune roll usage, weight_decay, dropout p, patience, then r_std scheme and alpha. Keep the best-scoring model; only submit changes you believe raise skill. Persist until submit budget is spent.
- Report best skill, chosen r_std scheme, final per-channel alpha, and which configuration (regularized vs champion-config) won on held-out.

Rules: only numpy + torch. Respect time_budget_s exactly (overrunning is penalized). Guard NaN/Inf. Report results clearly.
domain_knowledge: Predict the residual (Y - X) and standardize it. Prefer PER-(channel, latitude-row) residual std r_std[c,lat]=std over (samples,lon) of (Ytr-Xtr) with a small floor; residual variance is much larger in mid-latitudes than tropics/poles. Broadcast as [1,C,H,1].
Keep a per-channel scalar r_std as fallback and pick per-(channel,lat) vs scalar on the held-out split by lower cos(lat)-weighted RMSE. Never reuse the INPUT std for the target.

At inference: pred = X + alpha[c] * (r_std * net_output_standardized). Use a PER-CHANNEL trainable shrinkage alpha[c] in [0,1] via sigmoid (init ~0.5); optionally grid-search alpha on held-out after training.
Loss must be cos(lat)-weighted MSE (weights normalized to mean 1 over 32 latitude rows), applied on standardized residuals so channels and latitudes contribute equally.
Longitude (64) is periodic -> padding_mode='circular' on lon; latitude (32) not periodic -> reflect/zero pad.

Circular lon-roll augmentation: torch.roll X and Y by the SAME random shift along lon each minibatch; it preserves the residual and is exactly valid because lon is periodic. Never roll along latitude.

Use AdamW weight decay (default 1e-3, try 1e-4..1e-2) but exclude alpha and bias/norm params from decay via param groups. Add light Dropout2d (p~0.05..0.15) in conv blocks; too much destroys large-scale structure.
Early-patience checkpoint selection: evaluate held-out cos(lat)-weighted RMSE every K steps and keep the best weights; stop early if no improvement for P evals. Banking unused compute is fine.

NON-REGRESSION GUARD: also evaluate a champion-config (no aug/dropout, ~zero decay) on the same held-out split and keep whichever has lower held-out RMSE; if neither beats persistence RMSE, return persistence X.
Always de-standardize and add X back before returning; guard NaN/Inf and fall back to persistence X for any non-finite element.
Fill the compute budget: measure sec/step early, size steps to just under time_budget_s, reserve buffer for held-out eval and inference.
